🔑 1Password op:// refs — public-repo safety

chore/1p-public-ref-hardening · what a committed reference leaks, and how a placeholder keeps the template working while dropping the recon.

The core fact

✅ NEVER leaked  the secret VALUE. `op read op://…` needs auth to THAT
              1Password account. A stranger with the path gets nothing.

⚠️  IS leaked   in a PUBLIC repo: the METADATA — vault names, item
              names, account usernames → reconnaissance, not a breach,
              but avoidable and unnecessary.

Real ref vs placeholder — toggle

"args": ["-c", "TAVILY_API_KEY=$(op read 'op://Private/Tavily API Key/API Key') exec npx …"]
SegmentValueLeaks
vaultPrivateyour 1P vault taxonomy
itemTavily API Keya Tavily key exists
fieldAPI Keygeneric
"args": ["-c", "TAVILY_API_KEY=$(op read 'op://<vault>/Tavily API Key/API Key') exec npx …"]
SegmentValueLeaks
vault<vault>nothing — user substitutes
itemTavily API Keya naming convention, not org-specific
fieldAPI Keygeneric

Copy it literally → op read 'op://<vault>/…' fails loudly, forcing the user to fill in their own vault. That's the point.

What this PR changed (6 files)

BeforeAfter
op://Private/Tavily API Key/…op://<vault>/Tavily API Key/…
op://Private/orchestkit/hook-tokenop://<vault>/orchestkit/hook-token
op://Personal/PyPI - yonyonaiop://<vault>/PyPI
username: yonyonai<your-pypi-username>

Untouched: the op:\/\/ detection regex in mcp-health-check.ts (functional) and the gitignored .mcp.json (the maintainer's real config — already local).

Try it: paste a ref, see what it leaks

Generated for chore/1p-public-ref-hardening · secret VALUES are never in git — this is about metadata recon in a public repo.
